# HackXperience 2026

## About
HackXperience 2026 is a student-run hackathon organised by SIM IT Club (SIMITC), a student-led technology club under SIM Student Life at Singapore Institute of Management. The event challenges student teams to build real web applications solving real-world problems over a 24-hour period (non-overnight), with mentorship, workshops, and judging by industry professionals.

## Event Details
- Date: 22–23 May 2026 (tentative)
- Duration: 24 hours (non-overnight)
- Venue: Singapore Institute of Management (SIM), 461 Clementi Road, Singapore 599491
- Organiser: SIM IT Club
- Contact: it@mymail.sim.edu.sg
- Telegram community: https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl
- Telegram contact: @FukutaroJFS
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/simitclub
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/sim-information-technology-club/
- Website: https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app

## Pre-Events
- IT Club Project Showcase: 15 April 2026, 12:00–16:00, SIM Student Hub, Block B Level 1
- React & Next.js Workshop (2-part): Dates TBA, 19:00–22:00, SIM Campus

## Participation
- Expected participants: ~150 students across 30–38 teams
- Team size: 4–5 members
- Who can join: All SIM students, all years welcome
- Participant profiles: Web Developers, Data/AI Engineers, UI/UX Designers, Cybersecurity, Product Managers, Business Analysts
- Registration fee: Free

## Problem Statement Tracks
HackXperience 2026 challenges teams to build working web or AI-powered apps. Sample tracks:
- Reinventing the Art of Gift Giving — build an app for gift discovery and group gifting
- Gamify Healthy Cooking — gamified app to make healthy eating community-driven
- AI for Productivity — AI-powered app to help people work faster and access equal opportunities
- AI for the Planet — AI-powered environmental app for forest monitoring, natural resources, or climate impact modelling

Problem statements are revealed during the opening ceremony on Day 1.

## Past Events
- HackXperience 2025: 90+ participants, 20 projects, winner of SIM Outstanding Event Award (Silver, 2025 SLAAN Award). Challenge tracks: DynamicWeb & Kitchen Copilot.
- Omnitool Hackathon: 95 participants, 20–21 projects.

## About SIM IT Club
SIM IT Club (ITC) is a student-led organisation under SIM Student Life. Mission: create safe spaces for students to learn technology, build projects, and grow as a community of builders. Tagline: "Your Arrow into Tech."

Programs run by SIM IT Club:
- ITCamp: Frontendamentals — 3-month weekly web development bootcamp covering HTML, CSS, Tailwind, React, Next.js, and Vercel deployment
- Industry Workshops — guest-led sessions with tech professionals
- Internal Projects — committee members build and ship real products
- HackXperience — annual hackathon open to all SIM students

## Pages
- Homepage: https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app
- Projects showcase: https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app/projects

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Registration
Q: How do I register for HackXperience 2026?
A: Registration details will be announced in the ITCommunity Telegram group (https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl). Join to be the first to know when registration opens.

Q: Is there a registration fee?
A: No. HackXperience 2026 is free to participate.

Q: Who is eligible to participate?
A: All SIM students are welcome. All years are eligible.

### Pre-Events
Q: What are the pre-events for HackXperience 2026?
A: There are two pre-events. The IT Club Project Showcase is on 15 April 2026, 12PM–4PM at SIM Student Hub, Block B Level 1. The React & Next.js Workshop is a two-part session (dates TBA, 7PM–10PM at SIM Campus). Follow the ITCommunity Telegram group (https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl) for date announcements.

Q: What is the IT Club Project Showcase?
A: SIM IT Club subcommittees spent months building projects collaboratively. On 15 April, they showcase what they built. Visitors can get inspiration on what a typical project looks like and consult subcommittee members on tech stack, process, and tips.

Q: What is the React & Next.js Workshop?
A: A two-part hands-on workshop taught by trained SIM IT Club committee members, covering React and Next.js — popular, industry-standard development tools. Designed to equip participants with web development skills and prepare them for the hackathon. Attendees who complete both sessions receive a free e-Certificate.

Q: Do I need to attend the pre-events to join the hackathon?
A: No, pre-events are not mandatory but are highly recommended.

### Main Event
Q: When and where is HackXperience 2026?
A: Dates are tentative. The hackathon spans 2 days on SIM Campus. Follow the ITCommunity Telegram group (https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl) for confirmed dates.

Q: What are the problem statements?
A: Problem statements are revealed during the opening ceremony on Day 1 of the hackathon.

Q: How will projects be judged?
A: Teams present their projects to industry judges. Winners are announced at the closing ceremony.

### General
Q: What is HackXperience?
A: HackXperience is SIM IT Club's annual hackathon — a 24-hour challenge with workshops, mentorship, and presentations. In 2025, it brought together 90+ participants across 20 projects and won SIM's Outstanding Event Award (Silver).

Q: How many participants are expected for HackXperience 2026?
A: Approximately 150 students across 30–38 teams.

Q: Who can I contact for more questions?
A: Email it@mymail.sim.edu.sg, join the ITCommunity Telegram group (https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl), or PM @FukutaroJFS on Telegram.
